ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT CALPERS MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES RETIREMENT SYSTEM'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE.RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE IS DENIED. PETITIONER PROPERLY VENUED THIS ACTION IN SAN FRANCISCO PER CCP SECTION 401(1) BECAUSE THE ACTION COULD HAVE BEEN FILED IN SACRAMENTO AND THE ATTORNEY GENERAL HAS AN OFFICE IN SAN FRANCISCO. RESPONDENT FAILS TO SHOW THAT "THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES AND THE ENDS OF JUSTICE" WARRANT A TRANSFER TO SACRAMENTO. (CCP 397(C) (EMPHASIS ADDED).) ORDER SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Ruling On Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-07-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Lina Balciunas Cockrell (916.446.5297; Lina@majlabor.com) of Messing Adam & Jasmine LLP for Petitioner.
John Shipley (john.shipley@calpers.ca.gov) for Respondent.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONERs KENNETH HALE and LAVONNE WOLF's Motion For Ruling On Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate is taken under submission.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

***Order signed after hearing.***

Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Oct-06-2020 Off Calendar

Motion To Change Venue
Off Calendar Dec-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES RETIREMENT SYSTEM's Motion To Change Venue.

Respondent California Public Employees' Retirement System's motion to change venue is denied. Petitioner properly venued this action in San Francisco per CCP section 401(1) because the action could have been filed in Sacramento and the Attorney General has an office in San Francisco. Respondent fails to show that "the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice" warrant a transfer to Sacramento. (CCP 397(c) (emphasis added).)

Order signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
